examples.title: Algorithm: Circular Queue
examples.description: Demonstrates the implementation of a circular queue
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CircularQueue
INPUT: array of size n, front and rear pointers
OUTPUT: none

BEGIN
 front = rear = -1
 WHILE TRUE
 IF enqueue operation
 rear = (rear + 1) % n
 IF front = -1 THEN front = rear
 array[rear] = new element
 ELSE IF dequeue operation
 IF front = rear THEN front = rear = -1
 ELSE front = (front + 1) % n
 return array[front]
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the implementation of a circular queue using an array and two pointers, front and rear. The enqueue operation adds an element to the rear of the queue, while the dequeue operation removes an element from the front of the queue.

examples.title: Python: Circular Queue
examples.description: Implements a circular queue using a Python class
examples.explanation: This Python class implements a circular queue using an array and two pointers, front and rear. The enqueue method adds an element to the rear of the queue, while the dequeue method removes an element from the front of the queue.

examples.title: C: Priority Queue
examples.description: Implements a priority queue using a C struct and a binary heap
examples.explanation: This C code implements a priority queue using a binary heap. The heapify function maintains the heap property, while the insert function adds an element to the heap and the extractMax function removes the maximum element from the heap.
